name: Nonmyeloablative HSCT
summary: Nonmyeloablative HSCT is normalized for relapsing multiple sclerosis progression evidence used as a high-risk stem-cell replacement boundary.
mechanism_summary: Uses reduced-intensity hematopoietic stem-cell transplantation to reset or replace immune-cell populations in disease-specific contexts.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Nonmyeloablative HSCT delayed MS progression but remains a high-risk boundary; Nonmyeloablative HSCT relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis RCT.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
